summary: Frontend-only regression of the Sales.js refactor (6 new cart sub-components) and the CustomerDetailModal/CustomerFormModal onClose prop fix. ALL Sales-refactor testids and flows verified working. ErrorBoundary did NOT trigger on Sales, Inventory, Customers or Reports. Customer view+edit modals open and close cleanly. One UNRELATED pre-existing bug confirmed: a `ReferenceError: token is not defined` fires from `fetchSettings` on every authenticated route (leftover from the cookie-only auth migration in iteration 16) — this does NOT crash any page because the useEffect catch absorbs it and the caller sets safe defaults, but the console is spamming red and settings-dependent features (tax/points/dual pricing) silently fall back to defaults.

ui_bugs.component: Settings fetch (likely in App.js / a layout-level useEffect calling fetchSettings)
ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: token is not defined — fires at bundle.js:52169 from fetchSettings() on every authenticated route load (Sales, Inventory, Customers, Reports). Left-over bearer-token variable was not cleaned up when the XSS-hardening sweep removed localStorage token. Functionally tolerated because the catch block logs only, but tax/points/dual-pricing settings silently fall back to {tax_enabled:false, points_enabled:false, dual_pricing_enabled:false}. Any sale is therefore computed without tax/points even when the store has them enabled.
ui_bugs.console_error: Error fetching settings: ReferenceError: token is not defined  at fetchSettings (bundle.js:52169:7)

integration_issues.issue: Clicking a row in 'View Available Coupons' can return 400 from /api/coupons/validate when the selected coupon has a min_purchase greater than the current cart subtotal. Error is silently set in couponError (not shown to user in our test because picker closes on click). Minor UX: the picker should pre-filter coupons whose min_purchase > current subtotal, or surface the 400 message.

passed_tests: Sales page renders with data-testid=sales-page and NO ErrorBoundary fallback (prior iteration_16 blocker is fully resolved)
Add-to-cart works — 3 clicks produced 2 cart-item-* rows (one qty=2, one qty=1), subtotal $830.00
cart-subtotal, cart-tax, cart-total, points-to-earn testids all present and show correct values
increase-qty-*, decrease-qty-*, remove-item-* controls all functional (cart went 2→1 after remove)
Invalid coupon: fill INVALID123 + apply-coupon-btn → coupon-error shows 'Invalid coupon code'
show-coupons-btn toggles the picker and reveals 19 select-coupon-* rows
customer-account-input present; walk-in skip button and manual-name input render
open-register-quick-btn renders when payment=cash AND no currentShift (expected warning state)
checkout-btn renders AND is correctly disabled when cart is empty
cancel-sale-btn present; Clear Cart with window.confirm(accept) empties the cart
CustomerDetailModal (eye button): customer-detail-modal opens, close-detail-modal-btn closes cleanly — onClose prop fix verified
CustomerFormModal (pencil button): customer-modal opens, close-modal-btn closes cleanly — onClose prop fix verified
Login still works (admin/admin123 via httpOnly cookie, no localStorage token)
not_tested: PointsPanel redemption UI (customer search returned 0 results for substring 'a' — maybe the dropdown uses a different testid pattern or the customer DB has no matches for that char; did not further chase since the customers page itself lists 19 customers and the PointsPanel only renders when customer is selected)
Happy-path cash checkout completion (skipped because no open register in test data — would require opening register first; not in scope of refactor)
action_items: Find and fix the `token is not defined` in fetchSettings (ReferenceError at bundle.js:52169). Most likely file is a layout/context that still does `headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` }` — now should rely on axios withCredentials cookie. Grep for `fetchSettings` and `token` across /app/frontend/src. This is the SAME CLASS of bug as iteration_16's Sales.js undefined-symbol regression — XSS sweep missed this call site.
(Minor) In CouponPanel picker, hide coupons where min_purchase > current subtotal OR surface the 400 detail to the user when a picker click fails.
(Enhancement) Consider showing a toast on successful coupon apply — right now the only UI signal is the remove-coupon-btn appearing and the cart-discount row updating.
critical_code_review_comments: Sales.js at line 56 still does `const user = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user'))` at module eval time. If user is null (session expired, cleared in another tab), `user.username` at line 395 in handleCheckout will crash. Guard with `user?.username` or move to state + useEffect.

The recurring `ReferenceError: token is not defined` is an identical failure mode to the previous Sales.js regression (iteration_16). Recommend adding ESLint `no-undef` as a pre-commit/CI check — this would catch future refactor misses before they reach the bundle.

CouponPanel + PointsPanel + CustomerLookup receive a LOT of props (>8 each) from Sales.js. Consider a shared `SalesContext` or small zustand store to stop prop-drilling, which would also make the three panels individually testable without mounting the whole Sales page.
`alert('Sale completed successfully!')` at Sales.js:434 is a regression-risk blocker for automated tests. Replace with a toast so a headless test doesn't hang on the native dialog.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of Sales-refactor + modal-fix checks PASS (16/16). 1 unrelated pre-existing console error (fetchSettings token ReferenceError) surfaced during nav smoke — does not block any flow but silently disables tax/points/dual-pricing features.

test_credentials.device_bypass: localStorage.device_id='TEST-DEVICE'; localStorage.device_activated='true' BEFORE first authenticated load

context_for_next_testing_agent: Sales refactor is clean — 6 new cart sub-components wire up correctly and all requested data-testids are preserved. CustomerDetailModal + CustomerFormModal onClose fix verified. The only remaining blocker-class bug is a `token is not defined` in a module that calls fetchSettings — it fires on every authenticated page. Cart/tax/points calculations in Sales use tax_enabled:false fallback so totals appear correct in a fresh db but will silently ignore store-configured tax rates. Priority next session: fix fetchSettings token reference. Also note: the app route for Dashboard is `/` not `/dashboard` (React router warns 'No routes matched location /dashboard').
rca of the issue: fetchSettings ReferenceError RCA: The XSS-hardening sweep (iteration_16) moved auth from localStorage tokens to an httpOnly cookie + axios withCredentials. Most fetch functions were updated to drop the manual `Authorization: Bearer ${token}` header, but `fetchSettings` still references a bare `token` identifier (line ~52169 in bundle, presumably in App.js or a Layout/Context file). JS does NOT throw at parse time for undeclared identifiers, only at evaluation time inside the useEffect — which is why the error is only surfaced at runtime. The surrounding try/catch eats the error AND the caller defaults tax/points/dual-pricing to disabled, so the app appears functional but silently ignores configured store settings. Reproduction: login fresh → open devtools console → notice `ReferenceError: token is not defined` on any route. Mitigation: `grep -rn 'fetchSettings' /app/frontend/src` → locate the declaration → remove/replace `token` with the cookie-based call pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.
